_doc: Declarative manifest of comparable states for the whole-game parity sweep (re/parity/sweep.py). One entry per state both binaries can be driven to. P0 covers settled MENU screens (draw2d + pixel); boot/loading/race/results land in P1-P3. Each scenario's `screen` is the logical nav-screen id used by BOTH sides: the RE side parks there via MASHED_GOTO; the original side pushes it via FUN_0043d2a0 under Frida. `determinism` gates how strictly the differ runs and whether the scenario feeds C4 targeting (nondeterministic = excluded). Tolerances/rotate_a defaults mirror re/analysis/parity_tooling.md's settled-screen recipe.

scenarios.notes: Single Player (Challenge Cup / Quick Battle / Time Trial)
kind-2 submenu (no sid-specific code yet per nav_coverage)
Player Color Select — 6 car tiles + colour swatches
kind-4 widget screen (31/33 lack handling 19/32 have)

_planned._doc: Not driven in P0. P1 = boot/loading pixel-only (boot_frame driver). P2 = in-race 2D HUD (nav_race driver). P3 = in-race 3D world (flythrough + startgrid, draw3d channel). Listed here so the manifest documents the full intended coverage; sweep.py skips any scenario whose driver is not yet implemented.
